raw_response:
{
  "communicative_function": "self-commitment to metacognitive flexibility, functioning as a corrective prompt against polarized thinking",
  "implicit_claims": [
    "the speaker can reliably detect when they are in an all-or-nothing frame",
    "situations that trigger all-or-nothing thinking contain partial truth on each side",
    "actively searching for such partial truths is both feasible and preferable to binary framing"
  ],
  "coherence_assessment": "internally coherent as a conditional resolution; no logical contradiction between detecting polarization and electing to seek nuance",
  "conditions_for_usefulness": "the speaker possesses accurate self-monitoring, the domain genuinely contains distributed partial truths, and adopting this stance improves judgment or reduces distress without incurring false equivalence costs",
  "conditions_for_misleading": "one pole is verifiably empty of truth, accurate detection of the cognitive frame is absent, or the statement is used to manufacture spurious balance where decisive asymmetry exists"
}
